summary: Ukrainian forces struck the Afipsky oil refinery in Krasnodar Krai with kamikaze drones on March 14, 2026, causing a major fire confirmed by NASA FIRMS monitoring. The 225th Separate Assault Battalion used FPV drones to eliminate a Russian unit after surrender attempts failed, while the 53rd Separate Mechanized Brigade destroyed a Russian tank camouflaged with trees and netting using an FPV drone. Russian forces countered with the "Yolka" anti-drone system, successfully downing Ukrainian fixed-wing "Hornet" strike drones, and deployed FPV drones to destroy Ukrainian unmanned boats near Tendrov Spit and the Dnipro River mouth. Ukrainian FPV pilots are experimenting with tactics such as targeting mounted troops and using dual strikes, while Russian forces have engaged in direct confrontations, including a soldier charging an FPV drone with a bayonet.
